title: Leerpad 5B · Modals in Practice
subtitle: V1 + V2 patterns and modal meanings

questions.q: 'Jan kan werken.' — which meaning does the modal carry?
options: Obligation — Jan has to work
Capacity — Jan is able to work
Permission — Jan is allowed to work
Ambition — Jan wants to work
questions.explanation: kunnen = capacity (can / be able to). The four modals each carry a different shade: kunnen = capacity, willen = ambition, moeten = obligation, mogen = permission.

options: Ik kan goed Nederlands spreken.
Ik kan spreken goed Nederlands.
Ik kan Nederlands goed spreken te.
Ik spreken kan Nederlands goed.
questions.explanation: Modal sentence structure: Subject [1] · MODAL [2] · informatie · INFINITIVE [end]. 'Ik [1] · kan [2] · goed Nederlands · spreken [end].' No 'te' before the infinitive after a modal.

questions.q: 'Els wil soep koken.' Now front 'Vandaag'. Which is correct?
options: Vandaag Els wil soep koken.
Vandaag wil Els soep koken.
Vandaag wil soep koken Els.
Vandaag soep wil Els koken.
questions.explanation: Time fronted → inversion. The modal 'wil' takes slot 2, subject 'Els' moves to slot 3, and the infinitive 'koken' STAYS at the end: 'Vandaag wil Els soep koken.'

options: moeten
kunnen
mogen
willen
questions.explanation: mogen = permission (may / be allowed to). 'Mag ik even storen?' = May I disturb you for a moment? Compare: moeten (must), kunnen (can/able), willen (want).

questions.q: What's the polite Dutch way to say 'I would like to take French level 4'?
options: Ik wil franse les niveau vier volgen.
Ik moet franse les niveau vier volgen.
Ik wil graag franse les niveau vier volgen.
Kan ik franse les niveau vier volgen?
questions.explanation: 'wil graag' is the polite Dutch equivalent of 'would like to'. Without 'graag' it just means 'want'. Add 'graag' whenever you'd say 'I'd like to' in English.

questions.q: Where does 'niet' usually go when negating a modal sentence?
options: At the very start of the sentence
Between the subject and the modal
Before the infinitive (or before a negated noun)
At the very end after the infinitive
questions.explanation: 'Ik kan niet komen.' — niet sits before the infinitive. For negating a noun, use 'geen' before the noun: 'Hij wil geen koffie drinken.'

questions.q: Which sentence is in the WRONG order?
options: 's Morgens moet ik mijn tanden poetsen.
Op zondag mag ik lang slapen.
Wij willen morgen uit eten gaan.
Ik wil thuis werken vandaag wil.
questions.explanation: Option 4 has the modal twice and the infinitive in the middle — completely scrambled. Correct: 'Vandaag wil ik thuis werken.' or 'Ik wil vandaag thuis werken.' The infinitive ALWAYS closes the clause.
